name: Cornmeal Empanadas
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the ground beef until browned and drained. Stir in the spaghetti sauce.
2. Mix together all filling ingredients: raisins, chili powder, brown sugar, onion powder, salt, garlic powder, cinnamon, and half of the cooked meat mixture.
3. In a large bowl, combine flour, cornmeal, and salt. Cut in shortening until the dough forms coarse crumbs.
4. Gradually add ice water and mix until the dough comes together into a ball. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
5. Divide the dough into two balls. Roll one ball on a floured surface to form a circle about 16 inches in diameter.
6. Cut the rolled dough into four equal wedges, each approximately 7 1/2 inches long.
7. Place about half of the filling mixture onto each wedge. Brush with beaten egg and water mixture.
8. Fold each wedge over the filling to form a semi-circle, pressing edges together to seal.
9. Repeat steps 5-8 with the remaining dough pieces.
10. Bake on a greased baking sheet at 400°F (200°C) for 25-30 minutes or until lightly browned and golden. Serve with additional taco sauce if desired.
